methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Safe Superintelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $32B

private.name: Physical Intelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

private.name: World Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B+

private.name: Nominal
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B

private.name: Photoroom
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Air Space Intelligence is a Series B-stage AI & Software company founded by Lucas Kukielka that has raised $50M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of nine private companies and one public comparable (Palantir Technologies, ticker PLTR) operating in the same sector and stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses total capital raised ($50M) as a proxy for post-money valuation and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage transitions, yielding $95M. The peer-median method benchmarks against five same-stage AI & Software companies: Safe Superintelligence ($32.0B), Physical Intelligence ($5.6B), World Labs ($5.0B), Nominal ($1.0B), and Photoroom ($500M). The median of this set is $5.0B, with a range spanning $500M to $32.0B.

The triangulated analysis suggests a valuation range of $81M (low) to $5.8B (high), with a median of $2.5B. The wide dispersion reflects substantial heterogeneity within the Series B AI cohort, where valuations depend heavily on specific capabilities, team pedigree, and investor appetite for frontier AI risk. Key uncertainties include whether Air Space Intelligence's positioning aligns with earlier-stage generalists (Nominal, Photoroom) or latter-stage frontier models (Safe Superintelligence, Physical Intelligence), and the degree to which recent market dynamics have shifted the median valuation of the peer set.
